editmore_vertUserAnalyze this YouTube video transcript and extract:

1. **Key Points**: Main takeaways and important information
2. **AI Concepts**: Any AI, machine learning, or technical AI-related concepts mentioned
3. **Technical Details**: Implementation details, tools, frameworks, or technical specifications
4. **Visual Context Flags**: Identify sections where the speaker likely shows something on screen (code demos, diagrams, UI walkthroughs) that would require watching the video to fully understand. Include approximate timestamps.

Format your response as structured JSON:
{
  "keyPoints": ["point1", "point2", ...],
  "aiConcepts": ["concept1", "concept2", ...],
  "technicalDetails": ["detail1", "detail2", ...],
  "visualContextFlags": [
    {"timestamp": 123, "reason": "Code demonstration", "context": "Shows implementation of..."},
    ...
  ],
  "summary": "Brief 2-3 sentence summary"
}

TRANSCRIPT:
Let’s face it, the Turing test has been passed. Ever since ChatGPT came out, you have no idea 
wether you are talking to an AI or to a human. So now, we need a new Turing test, 
which as Mustafa Saliman has propsed, is to see whether AI can make a million dollars. And although do I disagree with 
Mustafa on almost everything, especially on AI safety, I do think that the 
best indicator of intelligence in the age of knowledge work is indeed the ability 
to create value within our economy. So, in this video, we are going to show 
you something that has never been done before. Specifically, how we developed the first 
fully autonomous business with AI. At the end, we'll even talk with the client himself, 
so you can prepare for this new age AI economy and hopefully even attempt 
creating such a business yourself. Let’s dive in. [LOGO Animation] - [LOGO](https://www.notion.so/LOGO-689d5b795d4d4aa189ea226fae58d991?pvs=21) # Generative AI is a Factory So, I wanted to start this video by quickly 
highlighting one of the points Jensen Huang recently made in his keynote. He said that, up 
until 2023, the tech industry mainly processed data for other industries, but it did not generate 
anything by itself. For example, when you fly on a plane, there's a lot of tech involved in 
the background, like processing your ticket, storing your data, and so on, but the value itself 
is actually generated when you fly on the plane, not when your data is being processed. 
So, although tech does significantly increase the efficiency and allows airports 
to increase throughput by hundreds of times, it does not actually generate the value by 
itself or it did not generate it before. Because now, with generative AI, 
for the first time in history, the tech industry can actually 
produce value by itself. So, instead of primarily assisting other industries, 
we can now generate anything—natural language, actions, audio, video, 3D information, all 
of that simply in the form of AI tokens. However, what he didn't mention is that you can't 
simply create a factory, even a digital one, out of thin air. A factory needs to be 
built around a process. For example, a car factory has a conveyor line where 
raw materials go through a specific process until they emerge as a finished car. The same 
should, of course, apply to a digital business, which means that our digital AI factories 
must also be built around a specific process. This is what I'm going to show you in this video. 
This is basically what we do in our agency. We take a process from the client and then we convert 
it into a digital automated factory with AI. Right, so enough talking. Here's the process 
itself provided by the client. As you can see, it's quite large, and this is what we're 
essentially looking forward to when we are working with our clients. If you have some form 
of SOP documentation, if you've already built some form of a process and you send it to us, this 
is like gold because we can automate essentially almost anything. We can automate almost anything 
if you just provide us with clear documentation. And if you don't have documentation, we also 
have SOP templates that you can fill out. So essentially what this whole process entails is 
online ebook generation and marketing. Here the process starts with the user who signs up and then 
requests an ebook by providing a dream customer, a problem, and so on. So then the first research 
assistant will perform all of the research. It will find the pain points, it will find additional 
information that should be included into this ebook. And then it proceeds with working with 
other agents in order to actually generate and sell this ebook. So, there are quite a few things 
involved here. It's not just the ebook generation. This system can also post Google 
and Facebook ads. It can generate landing pages for this ebook and it 
can even trigger an email campaign. After we've reviewed all the requirements, of 
course, the next step is to scope this project. It's quite important to scope the project, 
although we do try not to focus too much on project scoping. I'll explain why in a bit. 
But yeah, here's the client portal. All of our clients get client portals like this in Notion, 
where they can track our progress and see all of the notes that we've made for them, so they 
can learn a bit more about the system. For example, if the client requests documentation or 
something, we will add it into the client portal. Here is the content hosting, for example, 
piece that shows how this system, specifically how the research agent and ebook 
creation agent are connected to databases. We're using Google Cloud for this. For 
landing pages, we're using Contentful, Google Ads. And for the email provider, we're 
using MailChimp. The hosting itself is on Vercel. In the beginning of the project, we always 
try to determine details like this in advance, although I do always recommend to all my project 
managers to not focus too much on project scoping, because typically, the scope for those 
agentic projects tends to change a lot during the development process. So that's 
why we want to keep it as agile as possible. And here we have the initial agency structure, 
for example, which looks something like this, where the request from the user comes in, 
then the ebook director directs this request to either an ebook creation process or ebook 
marketing process. We have an ebook generation agent, a research agent here with a conduct 
research tool and generate ebook tool. In the marketing process of this ebook, we also 
have an ebook marketing manager agent that talks to the final copy agent, ad copy agent, and 
email agent. All of them have their own separate specialized tools. As I always say, 
you do have to focus on tools the most, because all the heavy lifting must be performed 
by the tools, not by your agents. Here we also scope the functionalities for each tool and an 
example flow before we actually start coding. Okay so now let's go ahead and actually take 
a look at the code. Here's the code for this agency. As you can see, we're using 
seven agents and the agency structure looks something like this. This is how you 
essentially set up those communication flows. These basically match the diagram that I 
showed you earlier, where ebook director can communicate with different agents for 
marketing and ebook generation processes. Let's take a look, for example, at the ebook 
generation manager. This is the agent that actually generates the ebook and it has one 
tool, Generate Ebook Tool. As you can see here, we're actually executing ebook generation 
asynchronously. We're actually even generating an ebook image and then combining it all together 
and saving it to cloud storage on Google Cloud. I believe the function that generates the 
book itself is right here. As you can see, we're using quite a large prompt that essentially 
splits the ebook into chapters and then generates each chapter separately in order to avoid the 
completion token limit. This technique allows you to generate the book much faster. Secondly, 
it allows you to generate a lot longer content. Now let me actually show you a demo 
with one of my project managers, Ford. Hey there, I'm Ford. I'm a technical project 
manager here at Versn AI, and I want to take you guys through a quick demo of the ebook creation 
system or Eladdin. We're going to be interacting with the ebook director agent and let's just send 
a message, say, create an ebook for entrepreneurs wanting to get into the AI industry in 2024. We'll 
click send on this and then this sends the message to the ebook director agent, which will determine 
the process that it needs to go through to create the ebook, which in this case determines that it 
needs to send something to the ebook generation manager to create the ebook. The ebook generation 
manager will now go in there and determine, hey, I need to do some research to do this. So 
it sends that message to the research agent to conduct research based off of the target 
audience of entrepreneurs looking to enter the AI industry in 2024. This then is conducting 
research of this target audience and identifying their pain points. It returns us back to the 
ebook generation manager. As you can see here, here are the five pain points that it 
has determined could be valuable. The book generation manager will now determine 
which of these pain points is most likely to really hit home with this target audience. 
Then it will go on to create an ebook for this. This step can take a little bit of time due to 
the nature of it but when you really get down to ebook creation time, this is a pretty short 
timeline. We'll take a second to wait for it here. Awesome. Now that the ebook has been successfully 
generated, the ebook generation manager alerts the ebook director agent that the ebook has been 
created. Now the ebook director determines, OK, now that the ebook is made, I need to create all 
the marketing material for the ebook. This will consist of a landing page for the purchase of the 
ebook, an email list to sign those users up for upselling them more ebooks and other things along 
the way, and ad copy and deploying those ads to various platforms such as Google Ads, Facebook 
Ads, Instagram, whatever you can think of. Here the copy has been created and now we are sending 
it to our headless CMS Contentful. As you can see, it's been deployed successfully. We will go 
there in a second once everything else has been completed. Now the funnel copy has alerted 
the ebook marketing manager that it is completed. This is an important step to have the landing 
page created first as when you create the email list and ads, it needs to be able to point the 
possible prospects to the place to purchase the book. Now we'll go in there and it's creating 
ads, generating them all, and then also creating emails. This will create the email list and an 
email campaign and schedule those emails so that it can be sent out to people as they purchase 
the book. Now we can see that the ad copy agent has completed its steps and is alerting the ebook 
marketing manager that everything is done. We can continue to the next step. Now that we have the 
ebook landing page created, we can now go to the website for this, which is currently hosted on my 
local computer. There we go. We have "Navigating the AI Industry: A Comprehensive Guide for 
Entrepreneurs in 2024" as the name of the ebook. We have a very simple landing page we've built 
out here and then here is the copy of the book, "Unlock the Future of Entrepreneurship: Your AI 
Industry Guide for 2024," "Overcome and Seize the Opportunities in AI with Expert Insights and 
Actionable Strategies." Enter your email below to get your free ebook. The copy here is a little 
off. We're still working on this, but you can purchase it here. This then will go and create a 
Stripe payment link for this user to pay for this book. Then we will be redirected to the Stripe 
payment page. Really quick. Awesome. Here we are now. This is a test mode Stripe. Nothing too crazy 
here. Now as we click pay, this will process our payment through the Stripe test mode, and then we 
will receive an email to download the ebook and be rerouted to the thank you page on our website. 
So that we can then ideally upsell a customer here with something new or just say thank you and 
here's your book. If I come into my email here, we can take a look and here is my download 
for this ebook. Hello, your ebook is ready to download. I'll click the download ebook link. Now 
here we go. We have our 27-page ebook title page and quite a decent amount of content that we are 
able to then go through. Now in about five minutes and with very little human effort, you have a full 
ebook ready to be created, sold, and marketed for you with very, very minimal effort. While this 
is still just a proof of concept in the world of AI and in this project that this is not currently 
live, this really shows that people don't really need to be involved in some of these businesses 
that you can do in the internet world, such as ebook creation or let your mind go to wherever 
field you're in that you can generate an ebook and honestly generate revenue and money doing 
very little effort and having these AI agents run your entire business for you. It's really the 
beginning of something amazing here and here at Versend AI, we really try to push the boundaries 
of what's possible. This is a great proof of concept and one of our first projects showing that 
AI agents are here to stay and they are very, very powerful. Thank you for your time. Once again, if 
you want to work with us or anything like that, our website is [versend.ai](http://versend.ai/) 
and we are looking forward to hearing from you. Okay, awesome. So, thanks so much 
William for joining. As I said, I'm super excited about your project 
because most of the projects that we get in our agency are about automating existing 
businesses, whereas your project is rather about creating an entirely new business with AI. 
So that's what really got me excited here and I just wanted to get your perspective 
on why did you decide to build this entire business using AI rather than going like a 
traditional route and hiring people for it? Well, you know, it's interesting because I've 
always built businesses and try to automate them in the past and usually what you have to do 
to automate is you delegate it to some people, some contractors or you get software to do it, 
but you still have to kind of like connect the dots and put it all together and so they were 
still work. So you almost like semi-automated it and then you have to kind of monitor it over 
time and I was always wanting to fully automate a business for the longest time and when AI came 
out I was like it's the perfect opportunity and when I saw your program the AI Agent Swarm and 
how your agents can kind of be customized for different things, that's when I knew this was 
the time for us to try to actually try this. So, amazing. Yeah, I definitely agree. You know, 
right now we have extremely smart models. You know, those GPT models they can perform like 
most human tasks almost on the same level, but where they fall short at the moment is 
in actually making sure that they actually produce like quality outputs at scale because, 
you know, if you ask the GPT model right now to generate like a website, it's not gonna 
generate you like an entire landing page, it's just gonna generate like one section and 
say like, "there you go". And I think that's why I really appreciated what you guys were 
doing because your process was it almost like it helped the agents to think, right, and 
when you're a business owner you're trying to create this business, you got to think 
through every step of what you're doing. And I think your agents working together in that 
capacity had the ability to make good choices. So when you put it together it would kind of like 
the process was a very intelligent process to be able to build out all those things. And as you 
said, these models are being so increasing in their abilities so that once you build it, as 
long as they keep increasing these abilities, then your whatever you build starts getting 
better, better and better. As they build it out it gets better as well. So I really appreciate 
that about about what you guys are doing. Yeah, exactly. That's definitely one 
of the primary benefits of using agents as far as that's what I always tell people, 
that you can scale your system very easily. So how did you determine like this initial 
process? Well, it's interesting because we, like I've been a consultant, a business consultant 
for about 15 years, like I had worked for business consulting. And one thing that is consistent 
amongst all businesses is that it's pretty much the same processes over and over and over again. 
You know, marketing, sales, operations, team, finance, the whole thing. And I was thinking, 
well why don't we just take those processes and find a way of linking them all together. 
And so then I created that flow chart to say, okay if we did link all these different processes 
together, what would it look like? When I go to a business and I try to help them evaluate where 
they can do better or be more efficient or more effective, I'll most likely start with a big flow 
of how their entire business flows anyway. And I was thinking, well if we have that information 
already, why not just plug in the AI to be able to do the steps that you need to make it flow 
automatically instead of having a human being involved in it. So that's why I think the flow 
model was perfect because businesses by far, 80-90% of them is all process. It really is. And 
so we decided to start with some easy businesses to kind of show that they're just pretty much 
almost 100% process, like information businesses, to just go ahead and show that this is 
possible. And that's why it made it a lot easier just thinking in a process way to be able 
to build businesses step-by-step in that manner. So what's your future vision for this 
project and where do you see it go from here? Well I mean, and we're in the process 
right now just building a small model, like a prototype of it to show that it can 
work from beginning to end. And we're using an information model for that. And we're building 
it in steps. So the first step is to show that it can be done. And then once that's done, then 
we're gonna expand the scale of it. So it's not only gonna be able to build a business, it's 
gonna be able to scale a business too. So like let's say if I'm selling an information product 
and all of a sudden the AI has got it working and people are buying this information product, then 
that means that we can scale it up now and sell more of those information products by adding more 
elements to it. Like whether marketing elements, sales elements, sales funnels, so you can add 
more. So I envision a business that you can kind of validate in the beginning with AI and 
then once it hits a certain validation level, then it'll automatically scale itself. And then 
if it hits another level, then it'll scale even bigger. And my hope is eventually it'll be able 
to kind of put itself up for sale. So if you want to even sell your businesses, you can do that 
too. So that's my first vision. Just a business that can go from small to scale up to scale up 
all the way to sale or you know just to keep it as a passive flow of income because it's gonna 
run itself. And my other vision is that's with information product. I want to see if we can do 
it with drop shipping. I want to see if we can do it with e-commerce. I want to see it then after 
that manufacturing. Because like I said before, every one of these businesses works on 
processes. And like you said, if we can feed the right processes to the AI, it'll have all 
the information it needs in order to be able to do that. So my vision really is to automate, I 
mean to create the world's first 100% automated business and then these other businesses 
at least automate 90% of their workflow. So that it makes, oh my gosh, you know how 
much money you can save with that and how much money you can make with that. And again, 
it'll just change the entire environment of business going forward. Yeah, I absolutely 
agree with you. As an agency owner myself, you know, the human element is typically 
the hardest one to scale in any business. So just one last question. What was your experience working with our 
development team and Ford? Yeah, and I gotta tell you, I really did 
when first meeting you, talking to you and understanding or getting to know how much 
the vast knowledge base you have in this area, that was really reassuring. And then working 
with the program, his name is Ford, he's great, great guy. It was great because we just 
kind of collaborated together to be able to identify areas that we can, you know, that 
we want to put the agents in and how to be able to move her one step to another. And it was 
a really great experience working together, collaborating on this task and this project. And I 
like it too because I have areas where I'm not an expert in the programming side and then Ford, he 
helped me a lot to be able to understand how these things work so I can better make choices about how 
we're able to make this first prototype. So that was really helpful a lot. And then I think the 
other part too is he gave me also more ideas of things that we can do to make it better, like to 
make it even bigger, like that vision that I have. My thing is I had the vision, I 
just didn't know how to put the pieces together and I think your company 
really helped me to do that. Amazing. Thanks so much William for choosing us as your 
partner in the EI revolution. Yeah, me too. Thank you again for all your 
help. It's really been a big support in me seeing this vision 
of reality. So thank you again. Let’s face it, the Turing test has been passed. Ever since ChatGPT came out, you have no idea 
wether you are talking to an AI or to a human. So now, we need a new Turing test, 
which as Mustafa Saliman has propsed, is to see whether AI can make a million dollars. And although do I disagree with 
Mustafa on almost everything, especially on AI safety, I do think that the 
best indicator of intelligence in the age of knowledge work is indeed the ability 
to create value within our economy. So, in this video, we are going to show 
you something that has never been done before. Specifically, how we developed the first 
fully autonomous business with AI. At the end, we'll even talk with the client himself, 
so you can prepare for this new age AI economy and hopefully even attempt 
creating such a business yourself. Let’s dive in. [LOGO Animation] - [LOGO](https://www.notion.so/LOGO-689d5b795d4d4aa189ea226fae58d991?pvs=21) # Generative AI is a Factory So, I wanted to start this video by quickly 
highlighting one of the points Jensen Huang recently made in his keynote. He said that, up 
until 2023, the tech industry mainly processed data for other industries, but it did not generate 
anything by itself. For example, when you fly on a plane, there's a lot of tech involved in 
the background, like processing your ticket, storing your data, and so on, but the value itself 
is actually generated when you fly on the plane, not when your data is being processed. 
So, although tech does significantly increase the efficiency and allows airports 
to increase throughput by hundreds of times, it does not actually generate the value by 
itself or it did not generate it before. Because now, with generative AI, 
for the first time in history, the tech industry can actually 
produce value by itself. So, instead of primarily assisting other industries, 
we can now generate anything—natural language, actions, audio, video, 3D information, all 
of that simply in the form of AI tokens. However, what he didn't mention is that you can't 
simply create a factory, even a digital one, out of thin air. A factory needs to be 
built around a process. For example, a car factory has a conveyor line where 
raw materials go through a specific process until they emerge as a finished car. The same 
should, of course, apply to a digital business, which means that our digital AI factories 
must also be built around a specific process. This is what I'm going to show you in this video. 
This is basically what we do in our agency. We take a process from the client and then we convert 
it into a digital automated factory with AI. Right, so enough talking. Here's the process 
itself provided by the client. As you can see, it's quite large, and this is what we're 
essentially looking forward to when we are working with our clients. If you have some form 
of SOP documentation, if you've already built some form of a process and you send it to us, this 
is like gold because we can automate essentially almost anything. We can automate almost anything 
if you just provide us with clear documentation. And if you don't have documentation, we also 
have SOP templates that you can fill out. So essentially what this whole process entails is 
online ebook generation and marketing. Here the process starts with the user who signs up and then 
requests an ebook by providing a dream customer, a problem, and so on. So then the first research 
assistant will perform all of the research. It will find the pain points, it will find additional 
information that should be included into this ebook. And then it proceeds with working with 
other agents in order to actually generate and sell this ebook. So, there are quite a few things 
involved here. It's not just the ebook generation. This system can also post Google 
and Facebook ads. It can generate landing pages for this ebook and it 
can even trigger an email campaign. After we've reviewed all the requirements, of 
course, the next step is to scope this project. It's quite important to scope the project, 
although we do try not to focus too much on project scoping. I'll explain why in a bit. 
But yeah, here's the client portal. All of our clients get client portals like this in Notion, 
where they can track our progress and see all of the notes that we've made for them, so they 
can learn a bit more about the system. For example, if the client requests documentation or 
something, we will add it into the client portal. Here is the content hosting, for example, 
piece that shows how this system, specifically how the research agent and ebook 
creation agent are connected to databases. We're using Google Cloud for this. For 
landing pages, we're using Contentful, Google Ads. And for the email provider, we're 
using MailChimp. The hosting itself is on Vercel. In the beginning of the project, we always 
try to determine details like this in advance, although I do always recommend to all my project 
managers to not focus too much on project scoping, because typically, the scope for those 
agentic projects tends to change a lot during the development process. So that's 
why we want to keep it as agile as possible. And here we have the initial agency structure,

then the ebook director directs this request to either an ebook creation process or ebook

research tool and generate ebook tool. In the marketing process of this ebook, we also 
have an ebook marketing manager agent that talks to the final copy agent, ad copy agent, and 
email agent. All of them have their own separate specialized tools. As I always say, 
you do have to focus on tools the most, because all the heavy lifting must be performed 
by the tools, not by your agents. Here we also scope the functionalities for each tool and an 
example flow before we actually start coding. Okay so now let's go ahead and actually take 
a look at the code. Here's the code for this agency. As you can see, we're using 
seven agents and the agency structure looks something like this. This is how you 
essentially set up those communication flows. These basically match the diagram that I

marketing and ebook generation processes. Let's take a look, for example, at the ebook

tool, Generate Ebook Tool. As you can see here, we're actually executing ebook generation 
asynchronously. We're actually even generating an ebook image and then combining it all together 
and saving it to cloud storage on Google Cloud. I believe the function that generates the

splits the ebook into chapters and then generates each chapter separately in order to avoid the 
completion token limit. This technique allows you to generate the book much faster. Secondly, 
it allows you to generate a lot longer content. Now let me actually show you a demo 
with one of my project managers, Ford. Hey there, I'm Ford. I'm a technical project 
manager here at Versn AI, and I want to take you guys through a quick demo of the ebook creation 
system or Eladdin. We're going to be interacting with the ebook director agent and let's just send 
a message, say, create an ebook for entrepreneurs wanting to get into the AI industry in 2024. We'll 
click send on this and then this sends the message to the ebook director agent, which will determine 
the process that it needs to go through to create the ebook, which in this case determines that it 
needs to send something to the ebook generation manager to create the ebook. The ebook generation 
manager will now go in there and determine, hey, I need to do some research to do this. So 
it sends that message to the research agent to conduct research based off of the target 
audience of entrepreneurs looking to enter the AI industry in 2024. This then is conducting 
research of this target audience and identifying their pain points. It returns us back to the 
ebook generation manager. As you can see here, here are the five pain points that it 
has determined could be valuable. The book generation manager will now determine 
which of these pain points is most likely to really hit home with this target audience. 
Then it will go on to create an ebook for this. This step can take a little bit of time due to 
the nature of it but when you really get down to ebook creation time, this is a pretty short
